outcomes.title: Sample Outcome
outcomes.description: Simplify the sum of two monomials.

outcomes.title: Sample Image Outcome
outcomes.template:
Write an equation whose graph is shown in the following image.

y={{line}}

Sketch y={{line}}.

outcomes.title: RREF
outcomes.description:
I can execute the next 2-3 steps of
  Gaussian elimination by hand that are needed to work toward RREF
  (and recognize that additional steps are needed to achieve RREF), and
  identify which row operations were used.
outcomes.template:
A given linear system has been represented by an augmented matrix and Gaussian elimination has begun. The current state of the process is shown below. {{A}}

Execute the next two row operations that you would perform to continue the process of Gaussian elimination, clearly stating your steps by writing the explicit row operations you did. You should write at least two additional matrices to show your work.

{{Row_Op1}}

Based on your final matrix, what would be the next row operation you would execute to continue Gaussian elimination? Simply state the next row operation; you need not perform it.

{{Row_Op2}}

Without doing any further row operations, determine the number of solutions that this system has and explain your thinking.

{{nsolution}} {{Row_Operations}} {{Arref}}

outcomes.title: Number of Solutions
outcomes.description: I can use information about pivots to make conclusions about the solutions of a system of linear equations, and explain the underlying reasoning (using appropriate terminology) regarding the existence and uniqueness of solutions and consistency or inconsistency of a system.
outcomes.template:
Consider each of the following augmented matrices.For each matrix, (i) circle the pivot positions, (ii) determine whether there is a solution to the system and, if so, whether it is unique, and (iii) state whether the system is consistent or not. Explain your thinking for (ii) and (iii).

{{simplified_system}}

The solution set has {{count}}. The solution set is {{solset}}.
